This stage provides the system’s user-authentication entrypoints and the persistence machinery that makes login survive across runs. It sits at the boundary between startup/onboarding UX and ongoing account-aware operation: CLI commands, TUI onboarding, and JSON-RPC account requests all enter here, while storage, revocation, and token refresh support the rest of the app afterward.

At the top level, cli/src/login.rs drives one-shot login, logout, and status commands, while app-server/src/request_processors/account_processor.rs exposes the same account lifecycle over JSON-RPC and tracks in-progress ChatGPT login attempts. The login crate is the core implementation: login/src/lib.rs and login/src/auth/mod.rs assemble browser OAuth, device-code auth, storage, revocation, and error surfaces into one API. login/src/server.rs runs the localhost callback server for browser PKCE login; login/src/device_code_auth.rs handles the fallback device-code flow; tui/src/onboarding/auth/headless_chatgpt_login.rs presents that flow during onboarding.

Persistence is handled by login/src/auth/storage.rs, with backend selection from core/src/config/auth_keyring.rs and concrete keyring access in keyring-store/src/lib.rs. login/src/auth/bedrock_api_key.rs stores Bedrock credentials, login/src/auth/revoke.rs performs best-effort token revocation on logout, and rmcp-client’s OAuth files mirror these patterns for MCP server credentials.
